data.title: Lease End's revenue grows by 60% in 2025 with $73M savings for drivers, yielding $108M equity.
data.description: A notable increase in revenue has been observed by Lease End, with a remarkable 60% year-over-year growth in 2025. This growth has led to significant benefits, including $73 million in savings and $108 million in equity being unlocked for drivers.

data.title: JFK's New Terminal One seeks aircraft de-icing service provider through RFP.Ensuring safety for all flights.
data.description: In Jamaica, N.Y., on January 13, 2026, the newly built Terminal One at JFK Airport has issued a fresh Request for Proposals (RFP) to source a contractor for aircraft de-icing and de-icing fluid recovery services. Interested service providers meeting the criteria can submit proposals for ensuring the safety of aircraft operations at the terminal.

data.title: New TFX Thermoformer in TAUNTON sets speed/flexibility benchmark for high-volume producers with precision, control, higher output.
data.description: A recent development in Taunton, Massachusetts has introduced a groundbreaking Next-Gen TFX Thermoformer that is revolutionizing the industry. This advanced technology has set a new benchmark by significantly enhancing speed and flexibility for high-volume producers. The Precision Thermoforming capabilities of this innovative equipment provide better control over the production process, resulting in higher output levels. This development marks a significant advancement in the field of automated packaging, with the potential to transform operations for manufacturers.

data.title: Top Wall Street analysts update Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing price expectations ahead of Q4 earnings. They predict earnings of $2.71 per share and revenue of $32.62 billion. Company reported strong results in December-quarter, exceeding market forecasts. Share price is at $331.65.
data.description: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (TSM) is set to announce its fourth-quarter earnings before the market opens on Thursday, January 15. Analysts are anticipating earnings of $2.71 per share and revenue of $32.62 billion. The company, headquartered in Hsinchu City, Taiwan, recently delivered stronger-than-expected results for both the month and quarter ending in December, surpassing market expectations. Despite this positive performance, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing's shares experienced a slight decline of 0.1% on Tuesday, trading at $331.65. For more details and the complete article, readers can find the full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Report on Polymer Extrusion 3D Printing Markets 2025-2034 added to ResearchAndMarkets.com, highlighting trends, forecasts, innovations in market.
data.description: A recent report on Polymer Extrusion 3D Printing Markets from 2025 to 2034 has been made available through ResearchAndMarkets.com. The report delves into the analysis and forecasts of this market, particularly focusing on the era of low-cost upstarts. It offers insights into the latest trends, findings, and projections for the polymer extrusion 3D printing sector. The report includes an Excel file with a market database outlining forecasts and impacts derived from innovations, adoption rates, and challenges categorized by material, segment, application, geography, and vendor.

data.title: Dow Jones drops on surprise inflation report; Nvidia stock falls. Oil stock breaks out. - Investor's Business Daily.
data.description: On Tuesday, the Dow Jones Industrial Average experienced a decrease following an unexpected Consumer Price Index (CPI) inflation report. Nvidia shares also saw a decline during morning trading. The stock market today showcased the Dow's drop due to the inflation report, while a particular oil stock demonstrated a breakout. This information was covered in live updates on Investor's Business Daily's platform.

data.title: Turkey data center market analysis predicts Ankara will surpass Istanbul with Khazna projects, hosting over 30 data centers. Turkcell, Equinix, Turk Telekom are leading operators.
data.description: In Dublin, the ResearchAndMarkets.com platform now includes the "Turkey Existing & Upcoming Data Center Portfolio" database. Turkey boasts over 30 operational data centers, with prominent locations including Istanbul and Izmir Arabia. Key operators such as Turkcell, Equinix, and Turk Telekom hold significant capacity in the country. The emergence of projects from Khazna Data Centers indicates Ankara's growing importance as a data center hub, poised to outpace other locations.

data.title: US-based Banana Metals launches online storefront, offering 6061 Aluminum Blocks directly to CNC Machinists.
data.description: Banana Metals, a company based in the US, has begun offering 6061 Aluminum Blocks directly to CNC machinists in Reno, Nevada. On January 13, 2026, Banana Metals, a subsidiary of SendCutSend, revealed the opening of its new online store. This platform is focused on delivering top-notch services to customers, specializing in high-quality aluminum blocks.

data.title: Authorities seizing $200K in USDT linked to a $500K scam involving a fake crypto trading platform targeting a victim in Massachusetts.
data.description: Authorities confiscated $200,000 from a victim in Massachusetts, who had transferred over $500,000 to a presumed fraudulent cryptocurrency trading platform.

data.title: First Horizon Corporation (NYSE: FHN) to release Q4 earnings on Jan. 15. Expected to report 46 cents/share, up from 43 cents/share last year. Quarterly revenue estimate is $862.8 million, up from $824 million. Stock price at $24.17. Analyst ratings available on Benzinga.
data.description: Before the market opens on Thursday, Jan. 15, First Horizon Corporation (NYSE: FHN) is set to announce its fourth-quarter earnings. Analysts predict the Memphis-based company will report earnings of 46 cents per share, compared to 43 cents per share in the same quarter last year. The anticipated quarterly revenue for First Horizon is $862.8 million, an increase from $824 million in the previous year. Despite this, the company's shares experienced a slight decline of 0.2% on Tuesday, trading at $24.17. For more information and the latest analyst ratings, readers can visit the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga's platform. This data is available for sorting based on various variables like stock ticker, company name, analyst firm, and rating changes. To access more insights, visit Benzinga's website.

data.title: Austin-based managed security solutions provider Beyond Secure acquired by new platform NuView, facilitated by Viking Mergers & Acquisitions.
data.description: Viking Mergers & Acquisitions recently oversaw a deal in Austin, Texas on January 13, 2026. Beyond Secure, a company from Austin specializing in managed security solutions for regulated industries, was acquired by NuView, a newly-established platform focusing on managed IT and cybersecurity. The acquisition marks an important development in the industry, highlighting the growing trend of consolidation and strategic partnerships. The deal showcases the increasing value of specialized security services, particularly in regulated sectors. No specific financial figures were disclosed regarding the acquisition.

data.title: Crypto's future in 2026 depends on institutions, rates, and retail comeback after a lackluster 2025 with Bitcoin and altcoins.
data.description: The recent rally in Bitcoin in 2025 has been relatively subdued, indicating a shift in the market. The decrease in the altcoin cycle also suggests a change in the crypto market structure. Moving forward into 2026, the outlook for cryptocurrency is likely to be influenced by institutional investors, interest rates, and the return of retail investors. This shift in market dynamics signifies a significant change in the crypto landscape and highlights the growing importance of external factors in determining the future performance of digital assets.
